---
title: "About the Book"
---

# About This Book

![]( <bookcover/bookcover(2).png> ){.book-cover fig-alt="Book cover for GEOMETRY and the Art of Design"}

This textbook is written for anyone who is curious about geometry, especially non-mathematics majors.

Geometry appears in design, architecture, art, nature, and everyday problem solving. I hope you start noticing all the geometry around you! This course highlights those connections while building your confidence with core geometric ideas.

Use this text as your course guide:

- Read through the short concept explanations.
- Use index cards for key terms and formulas. Review those regularly.
- Study worked examples step by step.
- Practice with exercises.
- Connect geometric ideas to visual design.
- Create your own geometric art.

## How To Use This Text

Each chapter follows a predictable structure:

1. Learning goals
2. Key ideas, formulas, and definitions
3. Design and real-world connections
4. Worked examples
5. Practice and reflection

## Suggested Chapter Workflow

Go through the information slowly. Take notes and work through the problems.

The Exercsises area will have an active google doc link for FIT students and faculty.

## Learning Mindset

You do not need to be a math major to succeed in this class. Growth comes from careful reading, sketching figures, explaining your reasoning, and practicing consistently.
